# LangGraph Field Guide

> A concept-level field guide to the LangGraph codebase, written by Doc Holiday
> (https://doc.holiday) by exploring the LangGraph source directly. Each page explains
> why a subsystem exists, its central abstractions, and how it relates to its neighbors.
> Every doc page is also served as plain markdown at <page-url>/index.md.

## Pages

- [The Big Picture](/docs/the-big-picture/): the map of the LangGraph monorepo — builder, engine, persistence, and the platform boundary
- [Anatomy of an Invoke](/docs/anatomy-of-an-invoke/): one graph.invoke() traced end to end through the superstep loop
- [What Runs Next](/docs/what-runs-next/): the version-trigger mechanism — how channel_versions and versions_seen schedule nodes
- [Your State Compiles to Channels](/docs/your-state-compiles-to-channels/): how a state schema becomes channel objects — reducers, the channel zoo, and when each errors
- [Control Flow Is Channels Too](/docs/control-flow-is-channels-too/): edges, branches, Send, and Command — how topology compiles away into channels and tasks
- [Why Checkpoints Look Like That](/docs/why-checkpoints-look-like-that/): the checkpoint format's design rationale — snapshots plus version bookkeeping
- [Replay, Resume, and Idempotency](/docs/replay-resume-and-idempotency/): what durable execution guarantees and the side-effect contract
- [One Engine, Two APIs](/docs/one-engine-two-apis/): what @entrypoint and @task compile to — the same Pregel engine
- [About This Site](/docs/about-this-site/): scope, audience, provenance

## See also

- Official LangGraph documentation: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/overview
- LangGraph source: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph
